utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi , stephanie . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing okay . um , so i know the nurse talked to you about dax . i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , stephanie is a 49-year-old female with a past medical history significant for congestive heart failure , kidney stones and prior colonoscopy who presents today for an abnormal lab finding . so , stephanie , i called you in today because your hemoglobin is low . um , how have you been feeling ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: over the past couple of months , i've been really tired and dizzy . lately , i've been really just worn out , even just , you know , walking a mile or going to work , doing things that i've done in the past every day that have been relatively okay , and i have n't gotten tired . and now , i've been getting tired .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yeah . i , you know , the nurse told me that you had called with these complaints . and i know that we have ordered some labs on you before the visit . and it did , it c- you know , your , your , your hemoglobin is your red blood cell count . and now , and that came back as a little low on the results , okay ? so , have you noticed any blood in your stools ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no , i have n't . i did about three years ago , um , and i did a colonoscopy for that , but nothing since then .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , yeah . i remember that , okay . and how about , you know , do your stools look dark or tarry or black or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and have you been , um , having any heavy menstrual bleeding or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , not that i've noticed .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and any , have you passed out at all , or anything like that ? any weight loss ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no weight loss or passing out . i have felt a bit dizzy , but it has n't l- led to me passing out at all .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so , you endorse some dizziness . you endorse some fatigue . have you , but you have n't had any weight loss , loss of appetite , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . so , you know , let's talk a little bit about that colonoscopy . i know you had a colonoscopy about three years ago and that showed that you had some mild diverticuli- diverticulosis . um , no issues since then ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: nope , no issues since then .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and then i know that , uh , you know , you have this slightly reduced heart function , you know , your congestive heart failure . how have you been doing watching your salt intake ? i know that that's kind of been a struggle for you .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , it's been more of a struggle recently . i've been traveling a lot . i went up to vermont , um , to go , um , explore the mountains . and along the way i stopped at , you know , mcdonald's and got two cheeseburgers . and so , i , i could be doing better . i've noticed some swelling in my , my legs . um , but nothing too extreme that where i thought i should call .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and any shortness of breath or problems lying flat at night , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . and then in terms of the kidney stones , i know that you had those a couple years ago , as well . any recent flare ups ? have you had any , any back pain , flank pain , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing like that .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . any blood in your urine that you've seen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . um , okay . well , i know that the nurse did a review of system sheet when you came in . and we've just talked a lot about your , your s- your symptoms , you know , your dizziness , your fatigue and that type of thing . anything else that i might have missed , fever chills , any nasal congestion , sore throat , cough ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , i've had a little bit of nasal congestion just because with the seasons changing , i , i get seasonal allergies . but everything else has been okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . well , i'm gon na go ahead and do a quick physical exam , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so , here in the office today , your vital signs look great . your blood pressure is fine . your heart rates r- right where it should be , which is good , okay ? i'm just gon na do a quick exam . and i'll let you know what , what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so , your physical , physical examination looks fine . so , on your heart exam , i do hear a three out of six systolic ejection murmur , which we've heard in the past , okay ? and on your lower extremities , i do notice some trace to one plus pitting edema in your ankles , which is probably from the salt intake , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: mm-hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , we'll talk about that . i wan na just look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the echocardiogram . so , i just wanted to go over the results of your last echocardiogram , that was about six months ago . that shows that you do have the low pumping function of , of your heart at about 45 % , which is not terrible . and it does show that you have some moderate mitral regurgitation . so , that's that slight heart murmur i heard in your exam , okay ? hey , dragon , show me the hemoglobin . and here , this is the hemoglobin that i was referring to . it's low at 8.2 , okay ? so , we'll have to talk a little bit about that , all right ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so , let me go over a little bit about my assessment and my plan for you , okay ? so , for you first problem this new anemia , uh , i wan na go ahead and send off some more labs and anemia profile , just to see exactly what type of anemia we're dealing with . i also wan na go and refer you back to the gastroenterologist for another evaluation , okay ? hey , dragon , order referral to gastroenterology . so , they're gon na do , uh , probably do an endoscopy and another colonoscopy on you . um , but again , i wan na send off those labs just to make sure that it's not something else , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: for your next problem your congestive heart failure , um , i do think you're retaining a little bit of fluid . so , i'm gon na go ahead and start you on some lasix 40 milligrams once a day . i want you to continue you on your toprol 50 milligrams daily . and as well your , as well , as your lisinopril 10 milligrams a day . i really want you to watch your salt intake , okay ? get a scale , weigh yourself every day . and call me if your weight starts to go up , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: 'cause i might need to give you more diuretic .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and for your last problem your kidney stones , uh , i think everything seems to be fine right at this time . again , continue to watch your diet and stay hydrated . um , and i know that might be a little difficult with the diuretic , but do your best . uh , and give me a call if you have any question , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . any questions right now ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: not that i can think of .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , great . hey , dragon , finalize the note .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 49-year-old female with a history of congestive heart failure, kidney stones, and prior colonoscopy.
Abnormal labs.
The doctor asks how the patient has been feeling.
The patient reports fatigue and dizziness over the past two months, exacerbated by daily activities.
The patient's hemoglobin level is low.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed blood in her stools.
The patient denies current blood in stools but reports an episode three years ago that led to a colonoscopy.
The doctor asks if the patient has observed dark, tarry, or black stools.
The patient denies dark or tarry stools.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced heavy menstrual bleeding.
The patient denies heavy menstrual bleeding.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced syncope or unintentional weight loss.
The patient denies syncope and weight loss, though confirms dizziness.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced loss of appetite.
The patient denies loss of appetite.
The patient had a colonoscopy three years ago showing mild diverticulosis.
The doctor asks if there have been any gastrointestinal issues since the colonoscopy.
The patient denies gastrointestinal issues since the colonoscopy.
Congestive heart failure
The doctor asks how the patient is managing her salt intake.
The patient reports difficulty with salt intake due to travel and notes mild leg swelling.
The doctor asks if the patient has experienced shortness of breath or orthopnea.
The patient denies shortness of breath and orthopnea.
Kidney stones
The doctor asks about recent kidney stone flare-ups, back pain, or flank pain.
The patient denies recent kidney stone symptoms or pain.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed blood in her urine.
The patient denies hematuria.
The doctor asks about fever, chills, nasal congestion, sore throat, or cough.
The patient reports seasonal nasal congestion but denies fever, chills, sore throat, or cough.
The doctor declares the intent to perform a physical examination.
The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam reveals a grade 3/6 systolic ejection murmur and trace to 1+ pitting edema in the ankles.
Echocardiogram shows an ejection fraction of 45% and moderate mitral regurgitation; hemoglobin is 8.2.
The doctor will order an anemia profile and refer the patient to gastroenterology for endoscopy and colonoscopy.
Order anemia profile and referral to gastroenterology.
Initiate Lasix 40mg daily, continue Toprol 50mg daily, and continue lisinopril 10mg daily.
Patient to limit sodium intake, monitor daily weights, and report weight gain.
Patient to continue dietary modifications and hydration for kidney stones.
The doctor asks if the patient has any further questions.

case_description: abnormal labs; congestive heart failure;kidney stones;colonscopy
